title: Pilosella × pawlowskiellum
taxonomy: Pilosella × pawlowskiellum is a hybrid species within the genus Pilosella, which is a part of the Asteraceae family of plants. This classification is based on its morphology and genetic characteristics. The Pilosella genus is known to be a complex group, consisting of many species and hybrids resulting from interspecific crosses. The hybrid Pilosella × pawlowskiellum is often found in Central Europe, where its parent species are native. As a result of its hybrid origin, its classification can be somewhat ambiguous and may be subject to ongoing research.

intro: Pilosella × pawlowskiellum, a rare hybrid of Pilosella officinarum and Pilosella eichleriana, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The health benefits of Pilosella × pawlowskiellum include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
